data.acceptance:
1. Every field in `src/lib/schemas.ts` under `foolerySettingsSchema` and its sub-schemas (`registeredAgentSchema`, `agentsMapSchema`, `actionAgentMappingsSchema`, `backendSettingsSchema`, `defaultsSettingsSchema`, `scopeRefinementSettingsSchema`, `poolsSettingsSchema`, `dispatchModeSchema`, `poolEntrySchema`) has a concise `.describe(...)` call covering purpose, valid values/range, and default.
2. `.describe(...)` text includes worked examples for non-obvious shapes: agent-id convention `<vendor>-<model-slug>`; canonical pool step names (`orchestration`, `planning`, `plan_review`, `implementation`, `implementation_review`, `shipment`, `shipment_review`, `scope_refinement`); scopeRefinement placeholders `{{title}}`, `{{description}}`, `{{acceptance}}`.
3. New CLI subcommand `foolery config schema` prints a deterministic JSON Schema (Draft 2020-12) of `foolerySettingsSchema` to stdout and exits 0; output parses with `jq` and carries the `.describe(...)` text through as JSON Schema `description` fields.
4. New CLI subcommand `foolery config validate [path]` validates a TOML file against `foolerySettingsSchema`; defaults `path` to `~/.config/foolery/settings.toml` when omitted; exits 0 with "OK" on success; exits 1 on failure with a Zod-formatted error on stderr naming the exact field path and expected shape.
5. The generated Foolery launcher (produced by `scripts/install.sh`) routes `foolery config schema` and `foolery config validate` to the new commands; `foolery --help` (or `foolery config --help`) lists both subcommands.
6. `docs/SETTINGS.md` is fully rewritten to document every top-level setting and nested structure (field name, type, default, validation range where applicable, description, example) and explicitly states that `src/lib/schemas.ts` is the authoritative source.
7. No new silent fallbacks are introduced; validate failures throw with clear messages naming the offending field.
8. Quality gates pass: `bun run lint`, `bunx tsc --noEmit`, `bun run test`, `bun run build` — no new failures.
